title: Exercises: Data Pipelines for Quantum Computing

exercises.title: Build a Feature Encoding Pipeline
exercises.description: Implement a complete feature encoding pipeline that can switch between different encoding strategies.
tasks: Create an abstract FeatureEncoder class with encode() and get_circuit_depth() methods
Implement AngleEncoder that maps features to rotation angles
Implement BasisEncoder that encodes features as computational basis states
Create a FeatureEncoderFactory that creates encoders based on configuration
Add validation to ensure data dimensions match encoder requirements
exercises.expectedOutput: A flexible encoding system that can create different encoders based on configuration
hints: AngleEncoder should work with continuous values in [0, 2*pi]
BasisEncoder needs integer values that fit in the qubit register
The factory should validate config before creating encoders
Include meaningful error messages for invalid inputs

exercises.title: Implement Result Post-Processing Pipeline
exercises.description: Build a post-processing pipeline that converts quantum measurement results into meaningful outputs.
tasks: Create a MeasurementResult class to hold raw quantum results
Implement probability calculation from measurement counts
Calculate expectation values for single-qubit Z operators
Implement Shannon entropy calculation for result analysis
Build a pipeline class that chains these operations together

exercises.expectedOutput: A chainable pipeline that extracts probabilities, expectations, and statistics from quantum results
hints: Z expectation: <Z> = P(0) - P(1) for each qubit

exercises.title: Build an Adaptive Batch Processor
exercises.description: Create a batch processor that adapts its batch size based on execution performance.
tasks: Implement a BatchJob class to represent batched quantum circuits
Create a PerformanceTracker that records execution metrics
Implement adaptive batch sizing based on throughput and failure rate
Add retry logic for failed circuits within a batch
Create a BatchProcessor that coordinates batch execution
exercises.starterCode:
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Dict, Any, Callable, Optional
import time
import numpy as np

@dataclass
class BatchJob:
    batch_id: str
    circuits: List[Any]
    shots: int
    priority: int = 0
    created_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)

@dataclass
class BatchResult:
    batch_id: str
    results: List[Dict]
    execution_time: float
    success_count: int
    failure_count: int

exercises.expectedOutput: An adaptive batch processor that optimizes batch sizes based on performance
hints: Track both throughput and failure rate to calculate adjusted throughput
Use exponential moving average for smoother adaptation
Update batch size periodically, not after every batch
Include retry logic for individual circuit failures

exercises.title: Implement Data Validation Pipeline
exercises.description: Create a data validation system that ensures quantum pipeline inputs meet requirements.
tasks: Define a DataSchema class to specify expected data properties
Implement validation for numerical data (bounds, NaN, Inf checks)
Implement validation for shape and dimensionality
Create a comprehensive ValidationResult with detailed error reporting
Add support for custom validation rules
exercises.starterCode:
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional, Callable
import numpy as np

@dataclass
class DataSchema:
    name: str
    expected_shape: tuple
    min_value: Optional[float] = None
    max_value: Optional[float] = None
    allow_nan: bool = False
    allow_inf: bool = False

@dataclass
class ValidationResult:
    is_valid: bool
    errors: List[str]
    warnings: List[str]
    data_summary: Dict[str, Any]

class DataValidator:
    """Validates data against schema requirements."""
    
    def __init__(self, schema: DataSchema):
